# cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network_adapter (Resource)

Manages a network adapter (NIC) attached to a Public Cloud VM instance. Create, change-network and delete are asynchronous; changing the network and deleting the adapter both require the VM to be stopped. Attaching (create) is attempted hot but is not guaranteed on every network (hot-plug limitation).

To manage this resource you will need the following roles:
  - `public_cloud_vm_instances_management`
  - `public_cloud_vm_instances_read`
  - `activity_read`

## Example Usage

```terraform
variable "virtual_machine_id" {
  type        = string
  description = "The ID of the VM to attach the network adapters to."
}

# --- Private Backbone network -------------------------------------------

data "cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network" "lan" {
  name = "LAN01"
}

# Re-pointing the adapter to another network (changing network_id) and
# destroying it both require the VM to be stopped.
resource "cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network_adapter" "eth1" {
  virtual_machine_id = var.virtual_machine_id
  device_index       = 1
  network_id         = data.cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network.lan.id
}

# --- VPC network ---------------------------------------------------------

# A network with a non-empty `vpc` block is a VPC network.
data "cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network" "vpc_net" {
  name = "my-vpc-private-network"
}

resource "cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network_adapter" "eth2" {
  virtual_machine_id = var.virtual_machine_id
  device_index       = 2
  network_id         = data.cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network.vpc_net.id

  # Optional static IPv4, registered on the VPC private network. Only honoured
  # on VPC networks (ignored on Private Backbone, with a warning); when omitted
  # the platform assigns one. Write-only: the observed address is `ipv4_address`.
  ip_address = "10.0.0.50"
}

output "eth2_type" {
  value = cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network_adapter.eth2.type
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `device_index` (Number) The device index of the adapter on the VM (0 = eth0, 1 = eth1, ...). Immutable; there is no API to change it.
- `network_id` (String) The ID of the network the adapter is attached to. Changing it re-points the adapter (change-network), which requires the VM to be stopped.
- `virtual_machine_id` (String) The ID of the VM this network adapter is attached to. Immutable.

### Optional

- `ip_address` (String) A static IPv4 address to register for the adapter. Only honoured on VPC networks — it is silently ignored on Private Backbone networks. Write-only: it is never read back from the platform (the observed address is exposed as `ipv4_address`).
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `ipv4_address` (String) The observed IPv4 address of the adapter. Reported by the guest agent after xe-guest-utilities is installed; may stay empty for a long time.
- `ipv6_address` (String) The observed IPv6 address of the adapter. Reported by the guest agent; may stay empty.
- `mac_address` (String) The MAC address of the adapter. Populated after provisioning; may be empty until then.
- `network_name` (String) The name of the attached network.
- `provision_status` (String) The provisioning status of the adapter (`provisioning`, `provisioned`, `failed`).
- `type` (String) The network type (`vpc` or `private_backbone`), computed by the platform from the attached network.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String)
- `delete` (String)
- `update` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# A network adapter is imported by the composite id
# "<virtual_machine_id>/<network_adapter_id>" (both UUIDs). The write-only
# ip_address is not read back and is not part of the imported state.
terraform import cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_network_adapter.eth1 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111
```
